steps.title: Map variables to objects
steps.content: The problem defines $x$ as the number of triangles and $y$ as the number of rectangles. Therefore, the ordered pair $(28,67)$ means $x=28$ and $y=67$.

steps.title: Check the pair in context
steps.content:
Substitute the values into the stick-count equation:
$$3(28)+4(67)=84+268=352$$
So the pair represents $28$ triangles and $67$ rectangles using all $352$ sticks. The answer is A.
